question: q07
question_title: 学生、教师、学校、社区和国家影响
statement: Discuss the real-world impacts on students, faculty, schools, communities, and the nation during the transition and in the end state, acknowledging that change is hard.
methods: 显式列出 students、faculty、institutions、communities 和 nation 的 transition burden 与 end-state gain，并生成健康前沿图和政策 brief。

data_source.source_pdf: /Users/wuxiaojun/code/Math-Modeling-World/docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets/2021/Checking the Pulse and Temperature of Higher Education

access_equity.definition: Students can enter, persist, and complete higher education across income, region, gender, race, and first-generation status.

affordability.definition: Tuition, debt, living cost, and public aid do not block participation or completion.

degree_value.definition: Graduates gain durable employment, civic, and personal value from credentials.

quality_and_learning.definition: Institutions deliver strong instruction, student support, and measurable learning.

research_and_exchange.definition: The system supports research, exchange of ideas, and international student flows.

funding_stability.definition: Public and private resources are stable enough for long-run planning.

innovation_renewal.definition: The system can align around a shared vision, execute it, and renew itself over time.

parameters.source_note: Official PDF statement parameters only; national score rows are deterministic planning inputs for audit and replacement.

impact_rows.impact_note: lower debt, clearer pathways, stronger completion support
impact_rows.change_is_hard_note: Transition burdens are explicit because institutional change takes time and creates temporary reporting, budget, and workload stress.

impact_rows.impact_note: higher human-capital resilience and research competitiveness
impact_rows.change_is_hard_note: Transition burdens are explicit because institutional change takes time and creates temporary reporting, budget, and workload stress.

impact_rows.impact_note: better regional talent pipelines and local participation
impact_rows.change_is_hard_note: Transition burdens are explicit because institutional change takes time and creates temporary reporting, budget, and workload stress.

impact_rows.impact_note: requires reporting and redesign, but improves planning stability
impact_rows.change_is_hard_note: Transition burdens are explicit because institutional change takes time and creates temporary reporting, budget, and workload stress.

impact_rows.impact_note: more assessment work early, steadier funding and innovation support later
impact_rows.change_is_hard_note: Transition burdens are explicit because institutional change takes time and creates temporary reporting, budget, and workload stress.

impact_assessment.transition_reality: The plan is beneficial only if temporary burdens on students, faculty, institutions, communities, and national budgets are acknowledged and managed.

policy_brief: Higher education policy brief: Brazil has enough room to improve affordability, access, funding stability, and renewal while preserving degree value and research strength. The proposed healthy state is not a single ranking target; it is a sustainable operating zone where each dimension clears the health threshold and policy capacity is renewed over time. The transition should begin with affordability and completion supports, then lock in funding, quality, exchange, and innovation governance.

selected_nation_transition.vision: A more affordable, completion-oriented, research-active, and renewal-capable national higher education system.
